failure_modes.text: A system can keep reproducing old behavior even after intent changes because historical grooves are stronger than the latest plan.
failure_modes.source_quote: Works when a system keeps reproducing old behavior despite new intent.
failure_modes.mitigation: Map the switching costs explicitly: for each path-dependent behavior, estimate what it would actually cost to change, and compare that cost to the value of the alternative.

failure_modes.text: Path-lock can become a self-justifying excuse for not redesigning anything that is actually within reach to change.
failure_modes.source_quote: path-dependence conflicts with status-quo-bias (status quo bias) when a correctly diagnosed path-lock is used as an excuse to avoid redesign rather than as a genuine structural constraint.
failure_modes.mitigation: Challenge fatalism with a 'what if we started today?' exercise: design the ideal approach from scratch, then compare it to the inherited path to see what the gap reveals.

failure_modes.text: Historical commitment can distort evidence reading so decision-makers selectively notice what justifies staying on the current path.
failure_modes.source_quote: path-dependence conflicts with confirmation-bias (confirmation bias) when decision-makers selectively credit evidence that justifies staying on the current path rather than fairly evaluating the case for switching.
failure_modes.mitigation: Audit evidence reading for confirmation of the status quo: ask whether the team would interpret the same data differently if they were not already committed to this path.

premortem_questions.text: Which early choice is constraining current options more than people realize?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Most useful when early choices are constraining current options more than people realize.

premortem_questions.text: What current redesign assumption is treating the inherited estate like a clean scheduling problem when installed constraints already narrow the real path out?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Most useful when a migration plan treats the inherited estate like a clean scheduling problem.

premortem_questions.text: If we tried to change course now, which habits, standards, interfaces, or prior investments would fight us first?
premortem_questions.source_quote: It is a strong fit when habits, standards, interfaces, or prior investments are locking the system into the current route.

heuristics.text: Map inherited constraints explicitly so the team can tell the difference between present preference and historical route-lock.
heuristics.source_quote: Path dependence is strongest when the real driver is inherited architecture, history, or commitment rather than present-day preference alone.

heuristics.text: Stress-test redesign plans against installed customers, contracts, interfaces, and operating dependencies before calling the path reversible.
heuristics.source_quote: The plan underprices installed customers, contracts, interfaces, and operational dependencies that already narrow the real path out.

heuristics.text: Use path-lock diagnosis to target redesignable constraints rather than to excuse staying with the inherited route by default.
heuristics.source_quote: Path dependence conflicts with status-quo-bias when a correctly diagnosed path-lock is used as an excuse to avoid redesign rather than as a genuine structural constraint.

curation_notes.summary: Focused on inherited route-lock, redesign friction, and anti-excuse discipline rather than on broad historical narrative.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor migration-policy sprawl because the raw source is already strong on inherited constraint, switching friction, and anti-stagnation checks.
open_questions: The first failure mode compresses several adjacent source passages about old behavior reproducing itself despite new intent.

allies: The raw allies section is strongest on commitment-bias dynamics and scaffolded path formation, but only the cleanest current-vocabulary ids should survive Wave 3.
antagonists: The raw antagonists and tension sections are strongest on first-principles-thinking, status-quo-bias, and confirmation-bias.
structured_tensions: The source contains an explicit structured tension block that should survive into Wave 3 with bounded normalization.
